description:
OL 316 (REV. 7/2020) WWW
Autonomous Mode 
Conventional Mode
Additional information attached.
An unoccupied Zoox vehicle (Vehicle 1) was stationary in the right lane on northeast-bound Bryant Street at 6th Street in San Francisco, 
stopped behind traffic at a red light. After the light turned green, the Zoox vehicle initially remained stopped to yield to an active police 
vehicle making a right turn onto 6th Street from the adjacent lane. During the subsequent green light cycle, as the Zoox vehicle began to 
proceed straight along Bryant Street, two passenger cars, including  Vehicle 2, cut across its path from behind, attempting an illegal right 
turn onto 6th Street from a through lane. The Zoox vehicle braked hard, but contact was unavoidable, and Vehicle 2 clipped the Zoox 
vehicle’s left front fender. The front-seat passenger of Vehicle 2 exited to inspect the Zoox vehicle before returning to their car and leaving 
the scene without exchanging information. The Zoox vehicle then cleared the scene and returned to base. Upon inspection, the Zoox vehicle 
was found to have sustained minor damage. Police were notified, and no injuries were reported.
Print
Clear Form
